title: antirrhinum majus var. siculum
intro: Antirrhinum majus var. siculum, also known as the Sicilian snapdragon or yellow snapdragon, is a variety of the common snapdragon plant that is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its bright yellow flowers and compact growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides and flavonoids. The health benefits of Antirrhinum majus var. siculum include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
